desc: Windhoek is less a classic sightseeing capital than Namibia's practical nerve centre: a highland city where German colonial remnants, government buildings, useful hotels and car-hire desks come together before travellers fan out toward Etosha, Sossusvlei or the coast. It works best as a staging post with a couple of worthwhile half-days, not as the country's emotional high point in its own right.
hls: Craft beer and game meat restaurants

todo.name: Christuskirche and Independence Museum area
todo.desc: Use the central landmarks to orient yourself before wider Namibia travel.

todo.name: Katutura guided tour
todo.desc: Book a responsible local guide for township history, food and contemporary context.

todo.name: Joe's Beerhouse or local dining
todo.desc: Try Namibian game meat, German-influenced dishes or craft beer before heading remote.

todo.name: Daan Viljoen reserve
todo.desc: Use the nearby reserve for an easy wildlife-and-walking warm-up.

todo.name: Craft centre and Independence Avenue stroll
todo.desc: Use the centre for orientation, practical shopping and one grounded look at Windhoek before the huge distances begin.

todo.name: Early vehicle and grocery stock-up
todo.desc: Treat the city as the place to sort SIMs, cash, fuel strategy and self-drive basics before heading remote.

wifi.note: Usually solid in business hotels, lodges and cafe-heavy central areas, but it becomes much less relevant once you leave the city because Namibia's road-trip rhythm quickly outruns reliable coverage.

prac.fltNote: Hosea Kutako is Namibia's main international gateway, but there is no LGW or LCY nonstop and most UK trips connect through hubs such as Frankfurt, Addis Ababa, Doha or Johannesburg. The airport itself is well outside town, so first-night transfer planning matters if you land late. Namibia's main safari and self-drive season also pushes up 4x4, lodge and flight pressure together rather than in isolation.
prac.currency: Namibian dollar (NAD), pegged to South African rand
prac.lang: English official; Afrikaans, Oshiwambo and German are also encountered
prac.visa: British citizens now need a visa to visit Namibia. GOV.UK says that from 1 April 2025 UK passport holders can either apply online for an e-visa or use Namibia's visa-on-arrival scheme, with a fee of 1,600 Namibian dollars for travellers over 11, and your passport must have at least 6 months validity and 3 blank pages.
alerts: Distances beyond Windhoek are vast; plan fuel, water and daylight.
Car rental demand peaks in dry-season safari months.
Do not assume old visa-free habits still apply: the Namibia visa change is now a real planning step.
Urban safety is manageable but use taxis after dark.

sweet: March, September and October are usually the most useful Windhoek months, either side of the hardest dry-season bottlenecks and before the city becomes simply a hot logistics stop.
